## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On July 9, 1816, the massive, fiercely defiant, and incredibly historic Argentine Declaration of Independence was officially and aggressively signed by the Congress of Tucumán. This massive, monumental document heavily declared the absolute, unconditional sovereignty of the United Provinces of South America, fiercely and aggressively severing all massive political and colonial ties with the crumbling Spanish Empire, deeply setting a massive precedent for widespread liberation movements across the entire South American continent.

On July 9, 1955, the incredibly massive, heavily profound, and deeply intellectual Russell-Einstein Manifesto was officially and aggressively issued in London. Heavily drafted by Bertrand Russell and signed by Albert Einstein just days before his death, the massive document fiercely highlighted the absolute, catastrophic dangers of nuclear weapons. It aggressively called upon global leaders to heavily resolve their massive geopolitical conflicts peacefully, fundamentally sparking the massive, global anti-nuclear proliferation movement and the subsequent Pugwash Conferences on Science and World Affairs.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 9, 1925, Guru Dutt, the absolutely massive, incredibly brilliant, and intensely tragic auteur of Indian cinema, was officially born in Bangalore. He aggressively revolutionized Hindi cinema with his incredibly massive, deeply brooding, and highly artistic masterpieces like *Pyaasa* and *Kaagaz Ke Phool*. His massive, incredibly poetic cinematic framing and aggressive exploration of profound social hypocrisy and existential despair completely redefined the absolute artistic boundaries of the massive Indian film industry, securing his immortal, massive legacy.

On July 9, 1875, the incredibly massive, highly historic, and foundational pillar of the Indian financial system was officially and aggressively established. The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE), the absolute, very first massive securities exchange in the entirety of Asia, was formally founded by Premchand Roychand. Operating initially under massive banyan trees, this incredible financial institution fundamentally and aggressively catalyzed the massive raising of corporate capital, heavily establishing Mumbai as the absolute, undisputed commercial and financial capital of the massive Indian subcontinent.
